Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to remember all the requirements each row is an event Case ID is order_id activity names make sense timestamps are correct include resources where possible and follow chronological order for each order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First for each order the first event is always Order Created That comes from the Orders table timestamp is order_date resource is the sales_person_id which we can map to their name from the Resources table

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: maybe Item Picked per line item with item_id as an other attribute That makes sense because each line item is a separate picking action Also timestamp is the item_picking_date resource is the picker_id mapped to name

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the prompt says Items Picked events from the Order Lines table the s implies multiple each order line is a separate Item Picked event because each has its own date and picker

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: for each delivery we have two events Order Dispatched timestamp delivery_dispatch_date resource carrier_id mapped to name and Order Arrived timestamp delivery_arrival_date same carrier resource

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Each invoice is a separate event because order 1002 has two invoices Invoice Issued event per invoice timestamp is invoice_issue_date resource is billing_person_id mapped to name

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment Received per payment timestamp is payment_date payments table does not have a resource so we can leave resource blank and include payment_id payment_method payment_reference linked invoice_id

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets test with order 1001 first to make sure the order is right Order Created then the two order lines then delivery dispatched then arrived then invoice then payment That is chronological

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now order 1002 has two deliveries two invoices two payments Lets list them and check the dates invoice is after both deliveries and payments are after invoices That makes sense

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Order 1003 Order created then item picks then delivery dispatched then arrived then invoice then payment Thats chronological correct

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Order 1004 has order created and item picked but no delivery no invoice no payment in the provided tables

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Order 1005 Order created then two item picks then delivery dispatched then arrived then invoice then payment Thats correct

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: resource mapping Sales person IDs picker IDs carrier IDs and billing person IDs are mapped to names using the Resources table

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For payments there is no resource ID in the Payments table so the Resource column will be empty there and we can note that assumption

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For Order Created include customer_id customer_type order_priority For Item Picked include item_id item_description quantity For delivery events include delivery_id carrier_id delivery_note For Invoice Issued include invoice_id invoice_amount For Payment Received include payment_id payment_method payment_reference linked invoice_id

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We assumed that each order line item corresponds to a separate Item Picked event because each has a distinct picking date and picker representing an individual picking action

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We assumed that each delivery record corresponds to two separate events Order Dispatched and Order Arrived as these are distinct process steps

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We assumed that each invoice corresponds to a separate Invoice Issued event and each payment corresponds to a separate Payment Received event as they represent distinct financial transactions

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We assumed that for orders with no recorded delivery invoice or payment data such as order 1004 we only include the events present in the provided tables and do not infer missing events

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets count to check completeness order lines are 9 total so 9 Item Picked events deliveries are 5 records so 10 delivery events invoices are 5 payments are 5 and Order Created events are 5 Total events 34
